reports.title: UK Electricity Mix Weekly Report: Monday 6 July
reports.intro: A measured weekly report for EnergyMix.info using the available 7-day historical generation feed for Tuesday 30 June to Sunday 5 July.
reports.summary: The week from Tuesday 30 June to Sunday 5 July averaged about 23.7 GW of measured generation, with renewables averaging 49.2% of measured output. The strongest renewable day in the available data was Saturday 4 July, when wind, solar and hydro together averaged 66.9% of measured generation. Gas was highest on Tuesday 30 June and lowest on Saturday 4 July.
reports.takeaway: For flexible electricity use, the cleanest opportunities are likely to appear when wind is strong, gas is low and demand is not at a peak. This is the foundation for future EV-charging guidance, clean-electricity alerts and weekly newsletter summaries.
drivers: Renewables were strongest on Saturday 4 July, when wind, solar and hydro averaged 66.9% of measured generation.
Wind was the largest swing factor in the available feed, peaking at about 11.7 GW average output on Thursday 2 July.
Solar was strongest on Thursday 2 July, averaging about 5.1 GW across settlement periods after embedded-solar backfill where needed.
Gas averaged about 6.9 GW across the period, rising highest on Tuesday 30 June and falling lowest on Saturday 4 July.
Measured generation was highest on Tuesday 30 June, at roughly 27.2 GW average output.

cleanestPeriods.title: Best low-carbon proxy: Saturday 4 July
cleanestPeriods.body: Historical carbon-intensity aggregates are not yet part of this weekly report feed, so EnergyMix.info uses renewable share and gas output as cautious proxies. Saturday 4 July had the strongest renewable share at 66.9%.

cleanestPeriods.title: Lowest gas proxy: Saturday 4 July
cleanestPeriods.body: Gas generation averaged about 2.8 GW on Saturday 4 July. Lower gas periods are often cleaner, but the report does not yet claim this was the lowest-carbon period without historical carbon-intensity validation.

higherCarbonPeriods.title: Highest gas proxy: Tuesday 30 June
higherCarbonPeriods.body: Gas averaged about 14.7 GW on Tuesday 30 June. Gas-heavy periods are usually higher-carbon, but this is labelled as a proxy until historical carbon-intensity highs/lows are added to the report feed.

highlights.label: Wind
highlights.value: Highest average wind output was about 11.7 GW on Thursday 2 July.

highlights.label: Solar
highlights.value: Highest average solar output was about 5.1 GW on Thursday 2 July.

highlights.label: Gas
highlights.value: Highest average gas output was about 14.7 GW on Tuesday 30 June; lowest was about 2.8 GW on Saturday 4 July.

reports.methodologyNote: This report is generated from the available 7-day historical generation feed and validated against external public data. Carbon-intensity highs/lows and interconnector summaries should be treated as future additions unless they are present as validated historical aggregates.

yesterday.title: UK Electricity Mix Yesterday: Sunday 5 July
yesterday.summary: Yesterday, Sunday 5 July, measured average generation was about 19.3 GW. Renewables averaged 53.3% of measured generation, gas averaged 3.8 GW, wind averaged 6.2 GW and solar averaged 3.6 GW.

highestRenewableShare.text: Current measured 7-day high: 66.9% average renewable share on Saturday 4 July, based on the available historical generation feed.

highestWindGeneration.text: Current measured 7-day high: about 11.7 GW average wind output on Thursday 2 July, based on the available historical generation feed.

highestSolarGeneration.text: Current measured 7-day high: about 5.1 GW average solar output on Thursday 2 July, based on the available historical generation feed.

highestGasGeneration.text: Current measured 7-day high: about 14.7 GW average gas output on Tuesday 30 June, based on the available historical generation feed.

socialPosts.title: Monday weekly grid brief
socialPosts.body:
Last week in Britain’s electricity mix:

• Highest renewable share in the available feed: 66.9% on Saturday 4 July
• Highest average wind output: ~11.7 GW on Thursday 2 July
• Highest average solar output: ~5.1 GW on Thursday 2 July
• Highest average gas output: ~14.7 GW on Tuesday 30 June

The shift was clear: gas-heavy weekdays gave way to a cleaner, wind-led weekend.

Full report: https://energymix.info/reports/weekly/2026-07-06/

socialPosts.title: Wednesday explainer
socialPosts.body:
What does “UK electricity mix” actually mean?

For live grid dashboards, it usually means Great Britain’s electricity system: England, Scotland and Wales. Northern Ireland is part of the UK, but operates in a separate electricity market.

Explainer: https://energymix.info/uk-electricity-mix

socialPosts.title: Friday practical clean-electricity post
socialPosts.body:
The cleanest time to use electricity is not a fixed hour.

It changes with wind, solar, demand, imports and gas generation. Windy overnight periods and sunny middays can be much cleaner than still early-evening peaks.

Guide: https://energymix.info/cleanest-time-to-use-electricity
